title: zanthoxylum budrunga var. rhetsa
intro: Zanthoxylum budrunga var. rhetsa, also known as Indian pepper or toothache tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family and is native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like gingerols and shogaols. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum budrunga var. rhetsa include relief from toothache, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues, as well as potential benefits in reducing fever and alleviating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
